## Trump to Launch US Food Centers in Gaza
President Trump sharply criticized Israel’s handling of humanitarian aid in Gaza on Monday, calling the situation “real starvation” and pledging that the [U.S. will set up food centers to feed children](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL8GMiPTNhgCB5eQzaUXombczPSpwcj2DJr9-hX7C5u-kkr16t9tviEL74-_uJEva5x3OrNQKZTmV0YOJT7wC7MQHXjshAeLzKChU9opGOeALJ_Z1sGvRECmJt-6zrjB2MHWT6976PuFaLXt4toBxtC7ZRyWpSWjcR1q9lebdjC2Wltrmr5zI5ql7wAohNVUVlPgElPzP_JenrTKJ74pNfe41eKfpRy5Mn8qmCqYzvNs9uV-WVz5RWKSUMF0aVQwcU5IopCDuojKBdMbC8ddrTEGtbFwwfRRB74yZZXuOwK0tu3q5MNxsgjn3lVWPzZk-RlRfvXc28N3AAAA__-bF4Ps). Speaking from Scotland, Trump said images of emaciated children were undeniable and directly contradicted Prime Minister Netanyahu’s claim that there’s no hunger crisis. The Israeli military began [a limited pause in fighting](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuhDAQRL_GdEZmbTAULtLQXdqUaMELbGQMwr5Dl6-PyC-kG2mkGb339RgeyIHj8okbuTzvQ6QrBcqZzuGZkH3eF_zBFIgOPCLhwSEMoKCWykroBo8c3oMpvJuJgHRBrrK1tq02rSlWp8kDUAtItZ5Gq6jyjR_raeqQWttUBbt7TVnoKqWM0WVT2w712NQA5P2shVHkOe9n-b1zzCvN4b2_6CynfSuCW3M-ktAfAnoB_XVd5TSmm-LuBfR3FNBzOpGCPPCZSM68rJnjItfnhpEznoxRInt5wwroi9P54-IQODVWGLVsyOHvMP_L0svBbwAAAP__T-19vQ) in three populated areas of Gaza for 10 hours a day as part of a series of steps that it says would give the United Nations and other aid agencies secure land routes to tackle a deepening hunger crisis. Israel says it is prepared to end the war if Hamas surrenders, disarms, and goes into exile, something the group has refused to agree to.
## 100+ Degree Temps Hit Eastern US
More than [200 million Americans are under heat alerts](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOnDAQBOCnMZ2RvTYHFC4iRXSXJopSogWvuY2MjbDvTpenj8gr_PVIn2bm932-I0dO2w_cydWQ50TvEqlWOudnQfY1b_gXSyQ68EiEB8c4g4JOql7COHvk-Jlt410gAjINOd13ph-MHWzzcBg8BTTjYrQO5JXtKGA3II7BmtV0DbtLUz2MWilrTXvr-hHNcusAyPtghFXkueaz_ZM51QeF-MkvOts17010j1qPIsw3AZOACZf1WtBu-YoFTL9-Cpi0UXLnGDkneVA-IsnlxJVkeVOsdHLa5JqT58o5FfksAqZS8_kRZmIvzHcNVmsY9dCczh_viyq3Xli17cjxf5P6pfteDv4FAAD__x-2hFg) as scorching temperatures sweep from the Midwest to the East Coast. Parts of the Southeast, including Mississippi and Louisiana, could see temperatures climb near 120 degrees. Cities like St. Louis, Charlotte, and Tampa are bracing for several days of triple-digit heat with little overnight relief. [Smoke from Canadian wildfires](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO8yAQhJ8Gd0QEsLELir9xl7-90lrMkuwdNpzBiXxPf3Je4ZrVSjP6NDMft-kGFGm9_4cFbQ1pWvFVItaK27QXIF_THX6gRMQMeUXIFOMkhWy5MFwOkweKx6QbbwOiRNWgvZpWmV7pXjcPK3rtXIdz71Rw4ILstXDohsG3V4MhNGRPmjByuAqhtbp0rRlAua6VEr0PimmBnmraLp-J1vrAEI_0xO0yp6WJ9lFrLkz9Y3JkclyPnEo9JSbHE8zkKMz5n2cv_Oz39s0caOPfO0SqB89xXxashfsdeU18hhU8wcpfFH2gDXlZ0hcyOTab9flFMVLpDNPivgDFd5b6pwGfVv4GAAD__9KhhWE) is adding to the misery, lowering air quality across the Northeast. Vulnerable populations are urged to minimize outdoor activity, stay hydrated, seek air‐conditioned spaces, and monitor at-risk neighbors.
## List: 25 Biggest U.S. Landowners
A new [report highlights U.S. land ownership,](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp8GOiNnbDAULrahy7Zbogkek9kdfmQ7oOzTX-W-wu0_fefo_LlPd2ThbfmNK_kS92mjKwuVQml6ZeRQ9gX_YxaiA4-N8GCRCTS0SjsFwxSQ5T3ZOvhIBGRq8jfXGtcb29v66QdrI9hIvYnUDUM3u4cm3ZNx0OqobzX7z5t2MNy0ttY0XesGNI-uBaAQoqmspsBlT83fnbfypCjv_aTUzPtai3-WcuTK_KpgrGC8rqs5Ob9QZjy4oHAun2EFY8LtHwWFKyWeMStolWBaKBd1JD6xkBLcwn5tlLL6SFUw1smH42IRzp2rrF5WZPkmlx_lOj18BQAA___rF3_U) with a few dozen families and individuals now holding millions of acres nationwide. The Emmerson family leads the pack, controlling 2.44 million acres of timberland in California, Oregon, and Washington. Media billionaire John Malone and media magnate-turned-rancher Ted Turner follow closely behind, with 2.2 million and 2 million acres, respectively. While timber and ranching still dominate, deep-pocketed tech and finance figures like Jeff Bezos and Thomas Peterffy are staking big claims, making land a growing symbol of long-term power and strategy. [See the land ownership chart here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp8GOiNnbDAULrahy7Zbogkek9kdfmQ7oOzTX-W-wu0_fefo_LlPd2ThbfmNK_kS92mjKwuVQml6ZeRQ9gX_YxaiA4-N8GCRCTS0SjsFwxSQ5T3ZOvhIBGRq8jfXGtcb29v66QdrI9hIvYnUDUM3u4cm3ZNx0OqobzX7z5t2MNy0ttY0XesGNI-uBaAQoqmspsBlT83fnbfypCjv_aTUzPtai3-WcuTK_KpgrGC8rqs5Ob9QZjy4oHAun2EFY8LtHwWFKyWeMStolWBaKBd1JD6xkBLcwn5tlLL6SFUw1smH42IRzp2rrF5WZPkmlx_lOj18BQAA___rF3_U).

Check out their investment strategies in today's edition. | Trump to Launch US Food Centers in Gaza President Trump sharply criticized Israel’s handling of humanitarian aid in Gaza on Monday, calling the situation “real starvation” and pledging that the . Speaking from Scotland, Trump said images of emaciated children were undeniable and directly contradicted Prime Minister Netanyahu’s claim that there’s no hunger crisis. The Israeli military began in three populated areas of Gaza for 10 hours a day as part of a series of steps that it says would give the United Nations and other aid agencies secure land routes to tackle a deepening hunger crisis. Israel says it is prepared to end the war if Hamas surrenders, disarms, and goes into exile, something the group has refused to agree to. 100+ Degree Temps Hit Eastern US More than as scorching temperatures sweep from the Midwest to the East Coast. Parts of the Southeast, including Mississippi and Louisiana, could see temperatures climb near 120 degrees. Cities like St. Louis, Charlotte, and Tampa are bracing for several days of triple-digit heat with little overnight relief. is adding to the misery, lowering air quality across the Northeast. Vulnerable populations are urged to minimize outdoor activity, stay hydrated, seek air‐conditioned spaces, and monitor at-risk neighbors. List: 25 Biggest U.S. Landowners A new with a few dozen families and individuals now holding millions of acres nationwide. The Emmerson family leads the pack, controlling 2.44 million acres of timberland in California, Oregon, and Washington. Media billionaire John Malone and media magnate-turned-rancher Ted Turner follow closely behind, with 2.2 million and 2 million acres, respectively. While timber and ranching still dominate, deep-pocketed tech and finance figures like Jeff Bezos and Thomas Peterffy are staking big claims, making land a growing symbol of long-term power and strategy. . name: Ernest Lineberger
subject: Update from Ernest Lineberger, July 27, 2025
body:
Hi Friends of Lineberger for US Congress!

To quote an old song, “Thank you for being a friend” (sung by
Andrew Gold 1978). I would be no where without your support and well
wishes. It’s important because the 2026 election is our chance to do more
than all of the protests we’ve set up or joined, all of the phone calls and
letters to elected officials, and all of the watching and worrying about
what’s going on. Flipping this Red MAGA US House seat is a very important
part of putting America back on the right track for the good of the
people. Let me tell you where we are.

We see things going more in our favor every day. Our assets
include a lot of determination and a growing population of like-minded
people. The next 15 months before the November general election are
precious. We need to use it to get more name recognition with a variety of
methods. We are already using digital advertising at an affordable low
level. We have hosted and participated in multiple town halls, multiple
mass protests, and other events. I’ve been attending multiple city council
meetings and taking my opportunity to speak. Still more needs to be done.
More that costs money that we need to raise. And even raising money costs
money.

Here’s what we are doing to raise money.

- House parties where you can meet and talk with the candidate in a
more casual setting.

- Email fundraising campaign highlighting our campaign vs. the
horrible current events and issues.

- Text fundraising campaign.

- Keeping my Campaign website running with its available donate
feature.

- Being present at multiple functions to inspire donations and
volunteers.

What would we do with more money?

- Hire a part time campaign staffer to help get more accomplished.

- Buy supplies, including lit cards, signs, t-shirts, post cards,
etc.

- Sign up for a VAN account via the State Democratic party to
facilitate canvassing.

- Expand the reach and volume of our digital advertising within our
district.

- Increase the volume of email addresses in our national
fundraising population.

- Consider possibilities that have been out of reach like sending
mailers or hiring a billboard.

- File to get on the Primary Ballot (and pay the fee) in late
November.

- Hold more events throughout the district to make contact with
voters.

Likely you have heard about the Trump demanded Texas redistricting effort
that is ongoing. I plan to attend the public meeting in Arlington on
Monday, July 28th, and give the following testimony (limited to two
minutes).

“Hi, I’m Ernest Lineberger, a candidate for US House for Texas CD 26, a
retired naval officer, a veteran, an engineer with 29 years at Texas
Instruments in Dallas, a believer in ‘Love your neighbor and we are all
neighbors’.

I ran for the open seat in 2024 and got 36% of the vote. Since then,
Rep. Brandon Gill and I have been working to convince the voters that I am
the better choice. Mr. Gill is new to public service and seems aligned to
serve the wealthy and take from those in need. While my approach is
Putting the People’s Needs First.

This redistricting plan has elongated districts with tiny tails in urban
areas, and a distant massive amount of rural that make it very difficult
for House members to stay in touch and correctly represent. Equally
difficult for constituents to seek support. Old districts are divided up.
End to end distance has dramatically increased. These seats might as well
be Texas At-large rather than district specific. This proposal is wrong
and harmful to our representative government.

The changes to CD26 are extreme. This will set me back in name recognition
and voter outreach. I’m eager to represent the voters of CD26, and be a
statesman of stewardship, who will look after their needs and those of the
nation. Water, Hospitals, Medicare, SNAP, VA, FAA, NOAA, FEMA and any
other assistance needed to help everyone reach their full potential. I’m
Ernest Lineberger of Denton County, I oppose this mid-decade redistricting,
but will serve my district and America regardless.”

Here is a link to an interactive website that shows the proposed new
districts.
https://dvr.capitol.texas.gov/Congress/0/PLANC2201?fbclid=IwQ0xDSwLouHxleHRuA2FlbQIxMQABHp5pZard7PRn_-rVHHt6qojMdrrjlyIHKLa4rFnoXD7wok2nZGEP-txYW1Sg_aem_FQdZbm_r8CoxdIjbBKQfLQ

For comparison, Here is the same type map of the current districts.

DistrictViewer <https://dvr.capitol.texas.gov/Congress/56/PLANC2193>

In other news, we held our first house party. It was a great success.
Good people, good discussions. We are looking forward to having more, and
are actively recruiting people who would like to host one. The goal is to
help more people get to know me as their candidate, as well as to collect
donations to help fuel the campaign to success.

Also, this weekend, 4th term Texas Representative, Vikki Goodwin was
hosting events in the area and I got to spend a little time with her. She
is now running for Texas LT Governor to replace Dan Patrick. That would be
a huge win for Texas. www.GoodWin4Texas.com

[image: image.png]

I get emails through the website. Some are people wanting to help and or
donate. Some are people with questions. One person, likely unhappy with
the state of affairs, wanted to know what I was going to do to win this
campaign. Here’s my answer.

“ Thanks for reaching out. I appreciate your message and resonate with
a lot of it. I have multiple things to say about your second paragraph and
still haven't decided how to respond. Maybe I'll start with the harsh,
strong reply. If you have been in CD26 for a while, do you remember the
name of the Dem candidate for US House in 2022? No, you don't because
there wasn't one. We were headed for the same story in 2024, no Dem
Candidate. Why? Because it is a hard seat to win and most candidates need
a job and can't afford to waste their time, money, life, and patience
putting in the work on a likely loss. Well, I thought that wasn't right.
It was wrong to hand the Republicans an unopposed win. It was wrong to
allow the Dem party in CD26 to further atrophy for lack of a candidate. It
was wrong to expect Dems to turnout to vote without a candidate. So, I
jumped in in late 2023. I had to learn it all from scratch with very
little help and even less money. I'm a retired naval officer and a 29 year
employee at Texas Instruments. I have three advanced degrees and very
senior qualifications in naval nuclear power and semiconductor
manufacturing. I'm a fast learner. The 2024 election came after less than
a year of learning and campaigning while still working full time, I got
over 138,000 votes. Brandon Gill had over $1.5 Million. My campaign did
all it could with less than $100K, about 1/3 of that from me. I lost, but
with no one ready to take the baton, I kept running on behalf of everyone
in CD26 (and the nation) who deserves a better representative than Brandon
Gill.

Today, with about 15 months to go before the general election, I'm
doing all I can to improve my name recognition, make more contacts, hold
events, raise money, plan a field campaign, and more on what amounts to a
microscopic budget. It might surprise you to learn that even though you
see some big names in the news frequently, it isn't easy for a candidate to
get much free exposure. Even raising money costs money. It becomes a huge
catch 22, but I'm doing all I can.

You would be surprised how many people want to offer advice or make a
demand. I welcome the input, but the demands are just like governmental
unfunded mandates. Very few say that they want to help, or that they have
a skill and time to help put to use to work towards a win.

So, your question was What will I Do? Short answer, everything I can
and have to do to try to win. I'll continue to hold events, fund raise,
run digital advertising to improve my name recognition, participate in
local events, and more. In late November, it will be time to file the
forms and pay the fee with the party for a spot on the primary ballot.
Whether there is a primary opponent or not, I'll grow my field campaign to
get out and meet as many of the voters as possible. That will continue
through the primary and end with the November election. Even I don't know
all of the specific events between then and now. We are planning to hold
multiple house parties for direct voter contact and fundraising. Tonight,
I'm going to an event to support Vikki Goodwin in her run for Lt Governor.
On Monday, I'm going to offer my support to opposition to the Trump/Abbott
mid-decade 'steal the vote' redistricting plan in Arlington. On Tuesday,
I'll attend my board meeting with Span/ Meals on Wheels of Denton County.
Next Saturday is busy with a plan to speak at a protest in Plano in the
Morning, man a table at a Community fair at Westside Baptist Church in
Lewisville, and if time permits, go to Sangers Farmers Market in the
evening to share my campaign message.

I'm not sure if any of this was helpful. I didn't go back and tell
you all that I've already done, including hosting three large protests and
speaking at two others. From now on, I've left my job to work this full
time, and everything has to either get votes or get money to help get
votes. The Trump 2.0 government empowered by the 119th congress and the
Supreme Court needs to be checked. Winning back the US House is the best
way to start and that is my goal. If you agree, I hope it's clear that we
need everyone's support, even if that just means spreading the word among
your circle. Check out the website. Sign up to volunteer.

Wishing you all the best.”

Putting People's Needs First! Giving you Value for your Vote!

Ernest Lineberger

Candidate for US House (Texas CD26)

LinebergerforUScongress.com <https://www.linebergerforuscongress.com/>

Lineberger for US Congress, PO Box 116668, Carrollton, TX, 75011-6668

To Opt Out from future emails – simply reply STOP.

body:
Tesla has agreed to a [$16.5 billion deal with Samsung](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAQhZ8Gd0QwdjAUFLdxl9tuaREzGLRjOzKTv7dfOdo32G6kOfp0vvN1GS-hUFnn_2FBz6-0jSs-KyEz7iNjpVDDUu_rHDFQve8PLERhBAVnqXoJboyh0HvsmuivKjrdoNf9ue2tsbZvstfGKrRoorZJW9QarqlLbUomRWXRNcUfMNWD01ppaE-gIRiHRqfWGacm0SmMhbf9xBkZX6Emem8P3E_TtjTkM_OtivafgEHAwDjlab-vUz7eAoaDLmBQ_XHbT6JSkLXMa5XayPNVHm7yWTjLX1nJm1zCN8pQ5JTLrQoYmt3H27MQlWp60al5CYU-Ffhvwz08_AQAAP__uKiA1w) to purchase its next-generation AI6 semiconductors from the Korean tech giant’s manufacturing facility under development in Taylor, which has been delayed due to challenges attracting customers.

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1878, a team of astronomers led by [Leonard Waldo](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2uqzAQxfHVmA5kDx-OCxevoctrb4kGPIClASJ7EsLur5It3OpfnV9xfu7DHSPHffmPG3l5z8ew05mZRCgNQpkx45af-xIIOT_TiyIzDqChLbUtwQ0BI19DUwQ_6uBMQd7Ytra37nazxeobsnYOY9fi2E6tbS0hOkPGTs6NMM9F9B9MW3DGaAN1BQawc9SZuXad05NqNIUoR6pkJaE35pmv40Wpmo6tYL-KPLKq_ynoFfTneVaSVzx2jjtVR1oU9N9VGfAqx-sTBT3tkj41xtVF8uFxRuaYO6savWwY-avL3z55efgNAAD__5k0dmI) of Harvard College Observatory converged on Fort Worth to [observe a total solar eclipse](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2uqzAQxfHVQGfkD8C4cPEaurz2lmiCB7A0YGRPSLL7q2QLtzvV70j_n9t0g0jxWP_Djp5fS5oOfBZCZswTYyEosJfHsQYEKo98YSSCSUvdCWmFdlOASO-prYO_y-BUjV7ZztihHwZbb96aFnrXalhUuHedbBW6zzb3QQZjbB39B5NWO6Wk0qbRSkPvsFeLcb2Tc9VKDJFTbnhDxheUhd7pwtzMaa_Jb8xnqcy_So-VHo_EeOFxQuEm5bXSI28o1GAHwYmBBM4Uz4ICjiC-mpgfOaYSOWKp9FhnH85nJIqlt1Ur1x0ifa_4b4Eur38DAAD__zM4exE). ## Flood Recovery Efforts Continue
Kerr County commissioners met Monday to [discuss a range of issues](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ_GdI7MkgAuKK6h-6-9Ejn2QlZZ25HXhOTtT9wrXDfSSPPNzJ-f5ccRU9p-u4hT_ax5SXgIY61YlorCTlyUPW0BHcte3kjMbgEDN20GDXYJjvi7XJsw3U2wbYNTO9y6YezHcWgeU-vdffV3RLh1rrsaO_oR7mhvxhjTGtvQdIaZAWzbmha6C7Tgeot9u3a2t8arq8FANZdLfWDFj5OVv_mN5eJzbHh61PoS1f1SMCuYj-O4PMXV01Qwn2sUzJy9YwXziVIwm-HUo4L5iaVon_dUvzqQ-F0ERa-cc9AF_Yn5al8oot5flfxTuxQ0-pxyJK_9wzFj2lA0Je1zjCRCOWERHRErpU3B3JQpvA5iJukHdTVbdMT_2tf_-_w9wd8AAAD__woRmD0) related to flood recovery. They approved a request to FEMA for temporary housing and the hiring of an additional investigator for the sheriff’s office to address an uptick in crime. Recently disclosed city emails indicate an electrical substation in Hunt was submerged under [eight feet of water](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbHOpDAMhJ8GOq9CYAkUFNfQ_ddeiQxxlug3ySox8O_bn3KvcM3IGlsefZo_X8sXevbh9RsPmuTHxSXQnZlEKC1CmTHjkc_wsoScz3SRZ8ZFK_0EZUCPi0XPn6Wr7bQqOzY1TY15tmboh8HU-7Rt2Jlu6NZtdOvQU2_V8Hx2trXb2rRG134qz5TRY9OoRrcP3WjsR-ob1479qLaqU2S9xPSQnYR-MDv-xIvSY4tHzdMu8s5V-6vSc6Xn-74f3xmlLCs9F5pKz8UBHy7K4l8oVLwSW-lZmTIX2c8gkM81C4qPAZicwBksJRjAEQlEBzcKJUBXdPfMsMUzSPqA4xhthkRbTDZD3uMNid7oUy43bEHwm-CIQfYSX6fJvm_P7HNvqk69DvT8D0n-r4hr0n8DAAD__2KqoCE). Repairs will likely take two to three months. While some summer camps [have reopened](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuOpDAURVcDmZFtPsaBg0nIatIJ0Rv7Ae7yB9kGit23qnoJnd4jHV2df4_5AdbZsP4Fj6q8ljgHvLLDUjDNBbODDD4fYTUILh_pROsczJzynlBBuJwNWHfPXW3Uf2okq1Ex0bdiHMZR1JtqmdaypUL3oOmIshfAeS96joPoqJa1VW8ZFVwyRhlvG844DBIHtrRykFRXHUVjS0xN2bDgC_Li7nhianT0tVNbKXuu2j8Vnyo-XdfVPF8Q3rDikw0GdwwGg0Zi4CaLi9HkDyFPTInoeIRykxw9knx4j4lo8HsmkJAkjDsGG1YCS8FEyobE4Am5QHmvX4e7SfcjrfhUJ2X2yzpn8yCqjq4erPvcLL-Leyr-HQAA___tyZF0), local companies that rely on summer tourism are [seeing depressed sales](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAQRL8GMiPbgA2Bg0vI9tILUa_dgLUNHtkNzPz9aeYXNq8q1Xv_vuYviBSP9S_s6Pi5pPnAuxAyY54ZC0GBvZzHGhConPnCSASzlroX0go9zgEiveauDu5bhlHV6JTtWzuYYbD15vrF9uj7Dr3qren8MAzGjENr0LcoVV9H9x6TVo9KSaXbRisNZkSjlnY0o_RVJzFETrnhDRmfUBZ6pQtz49Nek9uYH6Vq_1R6qvR033ezpKeFs3A83pFKT2-mSk-fstgikfDpPDi_BKczx7KLgvhThE87foP_EbAwZhHwgsLA8VjFQimFUmcXHnckisXYqpPrDpE-P_h39i6n_wcAAP__apKHIw). Meanwhile, flood survivors like L.A. Cud of Center Point are dealing with the aftermath of destroyed homes. “ _Fourth of July is not over for me yet_ ,” [he explained](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkT2O3DAMhU9jdxxI9I9GhYs07jZtSkNrUWNlaWkh0eOd2weeK6QhWBB834f352P5cJFjevx2O03yE_KS6KxMIlQWocquur0e6eHJcT3KkyKzW1DhAMoA2sW7yK-lb_30qbzVLU3aDJ25j_e7abeJaBg7HXyHvdPmPqDRdrCDUXfse9vrNk7XM2XQaq00djfU6EZLow6dHa1am16Rj5LLTTYS-nE18Cs_qdzWvLc8bSLftel-NTg3OJ_neVs5rl-45aNKTtdVg_Ol1eDMeXXc4HxlNjgrc-3XCPkoskEO8PfgF8QKKQtcMRBygZ3gmflIQlQqFNpdTLDmfY8i5EEyFPo8IvuYHnASfVVwQajAGxi2yAxrPpKUFwTO2V8wbZn89xmZYx1N06vH7iK_reT_unhO-C8AAP__Pquf8Q). In nearby San Antonio, the Spurs have joined 41 local and national professional sports organizations to [launch](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0E2OhCAQQOHT4K4NFCq6YDEbdz3bWZJSCiVB6UD13-0nPVeYC3zJez9Xd8WY4rl940GWXyG7k541ETMVx1QTVjzq_dw8Yar38qCYEjqQ0F-kucDkPMb0dl3j7SL9pBqyyvTajMM4mma3KvglkOlhCN1ojCFjei2DpnVdeq2WJtoPJg1MSkkFugUFOEw0qKCnYZKr6CT5yLm0vBPTC2tI7_yg0q75aJLdmW9V6C8Bs4C5IgZ_trlsAmZ-1VsuHHLZCT-ZAuamWH97xpRiHYzo5HZgTH8U_2_Aw8JvAAAA__8O53BF) the Texas Sport for Healing Fund. Over 150 pieces of rare sports memorabilia are being [auctioned](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUb3O3CAQfBq72xOsfzCFizTuvrQprcUsZxIMJ8Dn794-upPyBKlWq9HMaGZ-fa1f5IOP95908Fy_XVojXyVwrZzXyiVQoaOc8W6ZQjnzk30ItKLAAYQC1KslH15r39rZCKtly7NUQ6emcZpUu8-mI6ls1_Hg-qkfN7FNSgnetDDGscTWz28xoVBLKSR2N5RIo-ZRuk6PWmxNL9j6mvKt7lz5m4oLr_TkfNvS0YZ5r_VRmu5Hg0uDy3Vdtz8cy_BGG1woV78FbnApj5RraXCJhj7vmcu_C7-Tj1CZjgKBzrjtUHeGjxt8iOBShp3pXRa4M1qIhqBQBIo1RZ8aXFB1YMdJOakdiG4z0CsyMPVoALUe-tE4kj21ebaPy4fgy6iaXtwP8uGTpv7fBs8Z_wYAAP__b_CbGw) through next Friday to raise money for the fund.
## Austin Food Blogger Searching for Stolen Dog
Austin food blogger Taylor Hannan is [looking for his dog](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOozAQBOCngQ5kLwTjwsU1dP-1V6IFL2BpsRHehPD2p-QV_m6K0TfS_PsZfzBwiOtf3MnJe0ljpCszidA5CmXGjHt-xtUTcn6eLwrMOIKCR6VMBXb0GPge29K7SXmrS3LaPBrTd31vys1hD37RjxZ810-TNZbaSaMGvaBpTDuXwX0wZcBqrTQ0NWjAzlKnl8Z2Vs1Fq8gHSWctGwm9MS98pxed9Zz2kt0mcuSi-VPAUMBwXVe93xkjRkkxpE-pgIHDQllupgIGPCXM3_TVqiyJKVY-rVVMyTPlCpTpremgPrajPJ0_rsAccmeKVq07Bv5uy-8eezn4HwAA___YfIEN), a black terrier mix named Noodles, after his car was stolen early Friday morning with Noodles in the back seat. Hannan had gone inside his house while his car was running to retrieve Noodles' bed and food. He came back out just as a person wearing a black hoodie [got into his car](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL3OozAQAJ8GOkf-AWwKF9eky7VXoj28BkuLjbxOSN7-lHuFrx5pNJo_j-UBiVLefsOBvr1jWTJeTNga1qUhEzAc_MxbQCB-1hcmIli01KOQVuh5CZDoswx98H9lmFWPXtnRWDc5Z_vdg0E3OjUZZ6IJzho3jWbFOKIFZwbbJ_-VSatnpaTS5qaVhmnGSUUzT7Ncu0FiSK3UW9ux4Rs40qe8sN7WcvTk99ZO7syvTt87fT-xnIRf1Ol7ypGemFesghHquiOLWKo4EnPKmwhlExAbVrFCFRew4FYIs7hS28WJTaTMKaBQyjopjeurD-eViBJPthvkdkCi_x3tZ_deXv8LAAD__53thRg) and drove away. His gutted Honda Civic was recovered about 20 miles from his house. Hannan has owned Noodles for eight years after finding the dog on Craigslist. He filed a police report and alerted his social media followers. An organization called TRAPRS has also joined the search. "_He goes with me everywhere. He's just a good friend_ ," [Hannan said](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGupSAURb8GOwwcEKWgmMbuTTulQTl4yRzlRVDv_fuJ8wuv3FnJzt7rz9f05ROlff3tN3T1HfO0410Ia8VjqljIF7-Vc18DeirncWEi8hMI6LjoOdgp-ESfSTfBzSJY2aCTfaf6wQxD37wchrkHGIKwaBTouYsG9CzlYAQEJU2T3FMmerBSCgmqBQneWDQyKmusWJgWGFLNR1tfWPHtS6RPvvBol7w15F61fhemfjEYGYz3fbd_rxMfyGD0R00LIYPx-cVgpLx4esBZatp5zDnwtEc6cV_w4CGvvNRMuDMYwVgeotLaCMPFIJFri5YP8MQOpdUqRGnm5nDh-05EqZieabFuPtH_dfVnTi8H_wIAAP__2iOH_A). ## Tesla, Samsung Agree to $16.5B Deal
Tesla has agreed to a [$16.5 billion deal with Samsung](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAQhZ8Gd0QwdjAUFLdxl9tuaREzGLRjOzKTv7dfOdo32G6kOfp0vvN1GS-hUFnn_2FBz6-0jSs-KyEz7iNjpVDDUu_rHDFQve8PLERhBAVnqXoJboyh0HvsmuivKjrdoNf9ue2tsbZvstfGKrRoorZJW9QarqlLbUomRWXRNcUfMNWD01ppaE-gIRiHRqfWGacm0SmMhbf9xBkZX6Emem8P3E_TtjTkM_OtivafgEHAwDjlab-vUz7eAoaDLmBQ_XHbT6JSkLXMa5XayPNVHm7yWTjLX1nJm1zCN8pQ5JTLrQoYmt3H27MQlWp60al5CYU-Ffhvwz08_AQAAP__uKiA1w) to purchase its next-generation AI6 semiconductors from the Korean tech giant’s manufacturing facility under development in Taylor, which has been delayed due to challenges attracting customers. Tesla CEO Elon Musk said the agreement, which lasts through 2033, will provide the company with the hardware to achieve its long-held goal of developing fully autonomous vehicles. The chips will also power the company’s [Optimus humanoid robots](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2OgzAQBeDTQDfIHsCOCxfbpMu2WyKDBxjJ_Ag7OLn9imhvsP3M0_vez6N7OA68Tt9uIZte49atlGOglOjoEsXgolvic508uRCfx0kcgutQYAtCA5rOOw7vrim97YU3siQrdVvrm7rddDlbLdqhGQSpfsBG63GoDZp2RGOU7NvWl2yvMKHRSCkk1hVKdMqQkmNtlBFD0QjynLajSjMlerk4hvd20lEN21IGO6e0x6L-KvBe4D3nXPF6UkzbTp7ddVTg_UOByNMaQSpooecQeFvhckHmNMMfFMbtgJUyOIZh5j2ClFobU4vysH7P119UumjEtDgOnxLpf9OdFn8DAAD__8DKg0M). Tesla recently rolled out its self-driving Robotaxi service in Austin, but each of its vehicles is currently occupied by a human driver. The company is in the process of [expanding the service](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAUhZ9GOwzgCFJQbGP3b7uluQMXJbniBBgd337jvMLffclJvpxz_v3MPxAppuUvbGjrJ-xzwrMQ1op5rlgICmzlnRaPQOWdD4xEMEsuB8Y1k2b2EOmaH623T-6NaNEKPfR6VOOo29WC86AGDcr7oX-aEVwQqofeBeOC0q6N9pZxLY0QXMi-k0KCMqhE6I0y3DUPjj7WPXd1xYofKIGu_cDcuX1rya61vkrT_2nk1MjpPM_Opae7w0ZOt7uRE9c33_TdxF4EqbCQIyZfGCTPAmyRLlYwH9Ehi4k5oBj2nCKwjMuboO6ZFbhKt9aN2mz964xEsSjdPPiyQaRvo_q7Hw8r_wcAAP__LyiJAw) to San Francisco. For Samsung, the deal [enhances its reputation](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkM2upCAQhZ8Gd3QABWTBojcmM8md7SxNKWVLhh8j2F7ffmJn3mC2VScn5_t-f41f4INPr18Q0dbvJY8JzxKwVtzHiiVAgViO9HIIoRz7G30IMAomJGWaCjM68OEau8bZiTnDG7Rcy1b3qu91s9qFqb41spPSTczJdlGgl2XSfJ4nI7u-8fYuY1oYzhkX7UNwAcqg4ktrlGEz6Rg6X_P-qCtW_IayhCu_cX_MOTbBrrVuhbRPIgYihvM8H7Gk-0fEgIkehYgh5oQXEcOc4wbJ4337B0ZrphH-IP3QUvB0Xv1WqE-UKyrp5EPwOdF4hOovhJ3eKogYYKfPJ_-ZfzDd7NZt5x0sSpOOvSL48JlX_0_q24q_AQAA__-Ee4w3) as a competitor to Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC, which boasts a dominant 67.6% market share.

_**“SHE WILL NOT LET YOU DOWN”**_
Friend, in the critical upcoming special election for Florida’s U.S. Senate seat, I am honored to tell you that **I just received President Trump’s total and complete endorsement!**
**My name is Ashley Moody, and I’ve been serving in the U.S. Senate on a temporary basis since Marco Rubio became our top diplomat – _but now it’s time to win this seat outright. _**
I’m a fifth-generation Floridian, former judge and Attorney General, wife of a law enforcement officer, and mother to two wonderful boys.
W _e can win this seat, but to be honest, history is stacked against us._
**Typically, the president’s party averages a FOUR-SEAT LOSS in Senate midterm elections. If that happens, say hello to Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and goodbye to any forward movement on President Trump’s agenda.**
_I don’t need to tell you what a disaster that would be for this country._
**[President Trump put his faith in me to win this critical special election, and now I’d like to ask for yours. Our goal is 500 donations by 11:59pm tonight – will you pitch in now?](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aSLs/?__vbtrk=MzA2NjgxOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzA2NjgxOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: The fight to stop illegal voting starts NOW
body:
Save the Date: We’re rallying across America to demand that only citizens vote—see if we’re coming to your town.
| | | |
---
|
---
| Patriot-

---
| **** **** ******** The fight to secure our elections is ramping up--and we're taking it on the road. This August and September, Tea Party Patriots Action is launching the **Only Citizens Vote Month Bus Tour** --a bold grassroots effort to support the **SAVE Act** (Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act) and demand what should be common sense: **only American citizens should vote in American elections.** With noncitizens slipping onto the voter rolls due to loopholes and weak verification systems, it's clear we must take action--and we need to show Washington that the American people are serious about securing our elections. **SAVE THE DATES - The Tour Stops Are Set!** We're making stops across the country to rally voters, share the truth, and build pressure to pass the SAVE Act. If we're coming near you, we hope you'll join us in person! _Exact times and locations will be announced soon --**so mark your calendar now!**_
* **Aug. 18** - Cypress, CA
* **Aug. 19** - Las Vegas, NV
* **Aug. 20** - Scottsdale, AZ & Tucson, AZ
* **Aug. 21** - Las Cruces, NM
* **Aug. 22** - San Antonio, TX
* **Aug. 25** - Baton Rouge, LA
* **Aug. 26** - Jackson, MS & Montgomery, AL
* **Aug. 27** - Atlanta, GA & Greenville, SC
* **Aug. 28** - Goldsboro, NC
* **Sept. 2** - Bristol, WI & Orland Park, IL
* **Sept. 3** - Lansing, MI & Toledo, OH
* **Sept. 4** - Franklin Park, PA
* **Sept. 5** - Harrisburg/Mechanicsburg, PA
* **Sept. 8** - Annapolis, MD
* **Sept. 10** - Washington, DC
Our final stop in **Washington, D.C.** will be a major event: **we 'll be delivering your petitions directly to elected officials on Capitol Hill**, demanding that they pass the SAVE Act and protect the integrity of our elections before 2026. **Also, right now, you can:**
* [_Sign the petition_](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794ace3d17c/1edb9794ace0db38)
* [ _Let us know if you 'd like to attend one of the tour stops_](https://qcgp-cmpzourl.maillist-manage.com/click/1edb9794ace3d17c/1edb9794ace0db3a)
**This is our moment. Let 's make it count. _Let 's make history together._** Keep an eye on our website and your inbox for more details--and if we're headed to a city near you, we hope you'll show up and speak out for secure American elections where only citizens vote. I can't wait to see you! In liberty, Jenny Beth Martin **_The_** _**SAVE Act (Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act)** is a commonsense bill that would require individuals to provide documentary proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote in federal elections. It closes loopholes in current law that allow noncitizens to register and even vote simply by checking a box. The SAVE Act is a critical step toward restoring integrity to our elections and ensuring that only American citizens vote._ **** ****

# [A message to our Jewish parents on Gaza](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=169304359&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY5MzA0MzU5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTM3OTExMTksImV4cCI6MTc1NjM4MzExOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2Sphut5K240eua7U8Tm51NB5Xovo02QrEmWhLUnX61I)

Hey,
I'm writing to share a letter I wrote to my mother about Gaza.
While in some ways it's very personal, I published it in _[The New Republic](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/d75234b7-9863-4671-8478-aa6a614f22bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ , and am posting it again here, because it's an opening to a conversation I imagine many Americans--particularly Jewish Americans--have been trying, or struggling, to have with their families.
As Israel's campaign in Gaza, which former Israeli prime minister Ehud Olmert [describes](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/445003e0-8d58-4a9e-9c0d-cc8b5b2aaa1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians," reaches a new--though entirely foreseeable and long predicted--stage of mass starvation, I hope this can be useful to anyone out there who's decided it's time to have this discussion with their parents. Or, perhaps, to any parents out there who've decided it's time to try understanding their children.
* * *
Dear Mom,
It's time for us to talk about Israel.
There was a while, half a year or so after the Hamas attack on October 7, 2023, when we weren't talking with each other about much of anything. After months of sending dueling articles over family text threads on either the profound depravity or unfortunate necessity of Israel's war in Gaza, it became clear that--like many Jewish families across the country--we were talking past each other. So I mostly stopped engaging altogether. I just found it too bewildering, painful, and enraging to watch someone I love seemingly turn a blind eye to the indiscriminate death and destruction Israel was inflicting on Gaza.
But you're my mom, and I'm your son, so this silence was unsustainable. Eventually, the freeze in our communications thawed. All that was required to maintain our detente was one unspoken rule: We do not talk about Israel. My therapist certainly approved.
Yet the bewilderment, pain, and anger are all still there. As Israel's crimes in Gaza continue to escalate, and the unspeakable horrors of those crimes pierce deeper into our brains, these feelings have been getting harder to ignore. At some point, it will do lasting damage to our relationship, and I don't want that. So we need to talk about Israel.
Specifically, I need to express what it's doing to my heart to see you--and much of your generation--fail to grapple with Israel's ongoing genocide in Gaza. Because that is what we're talking about here. Gaza now has the [highest number of amputee children per capita in the world](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/d0537eee-f426-4a4c-b85a-9caf0fe5cc0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); Gazan [children are being purposely targeted by IDF snipers](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/b22986aa-b0ec-45fc-b1fd-66dd97f6fd88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); [all civilian infrastructure across the region](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/30ce1730-12b3-4bfa-9ab7-17c9323f86d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is being systematic levelled; [Gazans are being regularly massacred at Israeli-run food distribution sites](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/d14117ad-6fba-4ad1-b97a-e4fe521336ad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); and [mass starvation](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/1831164f-ec24-4a7a-8921-be4ec376fbc8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is underway. This isn't a war Israel is prosecuting, and hasn't been for a long time, if it ever was. What Israel is pursuing, [in the words](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/445003e0-8d58-4a9e-9c0d-cc8b5b2aaa1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of former Israeli prime minister Ehud Olmert, is "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians," not from "some disproportionate outburst by some soldiers in some unit," but through "government policy--knowingly, evilly, irresponsibly dictated."
And while campaigns of "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians" do not need to constitute genocide to morally necessitate opposition, there is a growing consensus--among those who know far more than we do about what is really happening in Gaza, and those who know far more than we do about genocide--that what Israel is doing in Gaza is, in fact, genocide. That is the conclusion of human rights organizations that have been operating on the ground there, from respected Israeli organizations like [B'Tselem](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/7141cf41-5540-4414-9fd1-6dadbd4adabe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Physicians for Human Rights Israel](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/de1411f7-4bda-45e8-b6b2-1736d11b4fcc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to international organizations like [Amnesty International](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/9600eb71-94a3-438f-9bd6-592849a290d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [Doctors Without Borders](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/35e241b6-da00-492a-a910-ace6bd91f12a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [Human Rights Watch](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/b045cf16-5d48-4935-908f-b5c4baa10878?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And it is the conclusion of genocide experts like the [president](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/989262c5-6d5c-41ef-be3b-0d61a1f5978c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the International Association of Genocide Scholars and the [editor-in-chief](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/83a5a7b6-8dfa-4dda-8ed1-39b010e779d8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the _Journal of Genocide Research_.
As Raz Segal, an Israeli professor of Holocaust and genocide studies at Stockton University, [put it](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/f2c5caa9-f37b-4b37-8619-3e25e1977c83?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "Can I name someone whose work I respect who does not think it is genocide? No, there is no counterargument that takes into account all the evidence." Or as Omer Bartov, another Israeli professor of Holocaust and genocide studies at Brown University, [wrote recently](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/85a30ab7-091e-4376-9f99-e291c222e89a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in _The New York Times_ , "My inescapable conclusion has become that Israel is committing genocide against the Palestinian people. Having grown up in a Zionist home, lived the first half of my life in Israel, served in the I.D.F. as a soldier and officer and spent most of my career researching and writing on war crimes and the Holocaust, this was a painful conclusion to reach, and one that I resisted as long as I could. But I have been teaching classes on genocide for a quarter of a century. I can recognize one when I see one."
"But what about the hostages?" you ask.
Yes, Hamas still holds 50 hostages captive in Gaza. But there is simply no way in which a moral or rational person's evaluation of Israel's atrocities should be impacted--not by one single iota--by this sad reality. Because--and I really can't stress this enough--_these are completely unrelated facts_. They are unrelated in function, given that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's administration has proven--again and again, beyond any shadow of a doubt, as articulated in the [clearest possible terms](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/76cb9bd2-f610-42a9-b59f-88ba27129ea2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the hostages' own families--that the Israeli government is not interested in freeing the hostages, and thus is not conducting its terror campaigns in Gaza in order to do so. Indeed, the man Netanyahu recently tried to install as the head of Israel's domestic intelligence agency literally [said](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/7f6e0451-e359-4720-bd38-ed5f04275fa0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "I oppose hostage deals. This is a forever war."
And they are unrelated in scale. Yes, it is tragic that 50 humans are being held hostage by Hamas. But that's less than half the number of humans that Israel [gunned down](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/b3b14274-e496-4e12-894b-efd97c24cd36?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) while they were seeking food aid over _just this past week_. Yes, it is painful to imagine the fear and hunger being experienced right now by those fifty hostages. But Israel is essentially condemning the entire population of Gaza to comparable degrees of fear and hunger. If anything, the hunger Israel is inflicting on Gaza's two million human beings is worse: I have not heard of any hostages starving to death, which is what Israel has already done to [hundreds of Gazans](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/3e964685-746d-4e54-b6f2-cdaae035e5ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and is in the [process of doing](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/1baf138a-b183-41cf-93ce-1f363963de22?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to tens or even hundreds of thousands more.
You also cannot brush away these facts by pointing to some other part of the world where people are being violently oppressed. Even if we were to assume, as you and many others often argue, that Israel receives disproportionate attention for the atrocities it commits, that _doesn 't in any way_ negate the fact that it is committing atrocities, nor does it diminish the urgent moral necessity of trying to end those atrocities.
But we actually shouldn't assume that the attention Israel is currently receiving is disproportionate to its crimes. I don't think there is anywhere in the world right now where people are facing more intense or widespread oppression than Gaza. Certainly, if I had to send my two sons, your grandsons, into a conflict zone, there is nowhere I would fear more for them than Gaza--not the Xinjiang region of China; not Sudan; not the Democratic Republic of the Congo; literally nowhere. What's more, none of the violence being committed against civilians in these other conflicts is directly paid for by my tax dollars as an American, or carried out in my name as a Jew. As such, I _should_ be giving this conflict my disproportionate attention, because I have disproportionate culpability in its commission.
This genocide is an all-of-government effort, and polling shows it is largely backed by Israelis. A recent _Haaretz_[survey](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e3645023-8f04-480b-b872-270f71b9c2a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that 82 percent of Jewish Israelis support the expulsion of residents of the Gaza Strip to other countries, with 54 percent indicating they were "very" supportive; 56 percent of them even supported the forced expulsion of Arab citizens of Israel to other countries; and, most chilling of all, 47 percent of Jewish Israelis said they agreed that the IDF, "when conquering an enemy city, should act in a manner similar to the way the Israelites acted when they conquered Jericho under the leadership of Joshua, namely, to kill all its inhabitants."
Yes, there are Israelis--including our Israeli family members--who strenuously oppose what is being done in Gaza (and who, it should be noted, are doing far more to protest Israel's government than me or most of us here in America). But it is clear that they represent a disturbingly [small minority](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/2cf60632-6ca7-46c4-8370-4109fdde919d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The reality is that Israeli society has, to a concerningly widespread degree, been radicalized, such that it is beginning to display characteristics of the societies that have facilitated genocides in the past--including, to just come right out and say it, the society that committed genocide against our people. And this gets to the real crux of my letter.
Growing up, every aspect of my Jewish identity was connected to the Holocaust: through stories we were told by Grandpa about his childhood in Germany; the trip we took with him to Treblinka and Auschwitz; books we read like _Maus_ and _The Diary of Anne Frank_ ; movies we watched like _Schindler 's List_ and _Life Is Beautiful_ ; songs we sang like "Ani Ma'amin" and "I Still Believe"; visits we made to the Holocaust Museum and Yad Vashem; and a million other everyday comments at Sunday School, Hebrew School, Passover, family get-togethers, and more. In so many ways, it was instilled in me that there's nothing worse than supporting the perpetrators of genocide, nothing more cowardly than staying silent in the face of genocide, and nothing more virtuous than standing up against genocide.
And I'm so grateful for this! It's a credit to you and our family and our Jewish community that "Never Again" was the morality that I was marinated in as a child and that has influenced so many of my choices as an adult. It's why as a state legislator I worked to pass--alongside an Armenian-American colleague--a [Holocaust and genocide education requirement](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/64a2b3dd-8b70-40cd-94b8-28fdcb2a9067?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for my state's public schools. It's why I've [fought attempts to turn away refugees](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/b6ffe99d-9f54-4c51-a2d3-0551a90afa9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that reminded me of America's failure to accept Jewish refugees during the 1930s. It's why I spent an evening in jail for engaging in [civil disobedience at an ICE detention facility](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/7ba2182e-0b4a-4043-8cf5-3c920728212d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that had been operating much like a concentration camp. This has been a through-line not just in my career, but in the activism and worldview of so many Jewish Americans who were raised right by our parents.
So when we see those same parents, the people we love most in the world, fail in this most elemental task--to clearly and unequivocally and loudly oppose a genocide (or even, if you still can't accept that terminology, an "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians" that strongly rhymes with genocide), let alone one _being done in our name_ --that is not something we can just agree to disagree about.
When I look at my two sons, I can't not see the children of Gaza--scared, orphaned, disfigured, and now literally starving to death--and feel profound shame that I have not done more over these past two years to stand against these crimes. Mom, you are a beautifully kind and empathetic person in so many ways. And yet, I don't believe that when you look at your grandsons, you have ever pictured them similarly.
But I need you to try. I need you to step out of your echo chamber for a moment. I need you to at least consider the possibility that what Israel is doing to Gaza is, in practice if not scale, what was done to our ancestors. In short, I need you to be the parent I have always looked up to. The role model who taught me right from wrong. The mother who taught me to say "Never Again."
Love,
Your son, Aaron

clean_body: You taught us to say "Never Again." We need you to say it now. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more You taught us to say "Never Again." We need you to say it now. | | | Jul 29 | | | | | | | | | | | | Hey, I'm writing to share a letter I wrote to my mother about Gaza. While in some ways it's very personal, I published it in , and am posting it again here, because it's an opening to a conversation I imagine many Americans--particularly Jewish Americans--have been trying, or struggling, to have with their families. As Israel's campaign in Gaza, which former Israeli prime minister Ehud Olmert as "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians," reaches a new--though entirely foreseeable and long predicted--stage of mass starvation, I hope this can be useful to anyone out there who's decided it's time to have this discussion with their parents. Or, perhaps, to any parents out there who've decided it's time to try understanding their children. * Dear Mom, It's time for us to talk about Israel. There was a while, half a year or so after the Hamas attack on October 7, 2023, when we weren't talking with each other about much of anything. After months of sending dueling articles over family text threads on either the profound depravity or unfortunate necessity of Israel's war in Gaza, it became clear that--like many Jewish families across the country--we were talking past each other. So I mostly stopped engaging altogether. I just found it too bewildering, painful, and enraging to watch someone I love seemingly turn a blind eye to the indiscriminate death and destruction Israel was inflicting on Gaza. But you're my mom, and I'm your son, so this silence was unsustainable. Eventually, the freeze in our communications thawed. All that was required to maintain our detente was one unspoken rule: We do not talk about Israel. My therapist certainly approved. Yet the bewilderment, pain, and anger are all still there. As Israel's crimes in Gaza continue to escalate, and the unspeakable horrors of those crimes pierce deeper into our brains, these feelings have been getting harder to ignore. At some point, it will do lasting damage to our relationship, and I don't want that. So we need to talk about Israel. Specifically, I need to express what it's doing to my heart to see you--and much of your generation--fail to grapple with Israel's ongoing genocide in Gaza. Because that is what we're talking about here. Gaza now has the ; Gazan ; is being systematic levelled; ; and is underway. This isn't a war Israel is prosecuting, and hasn't been for a long time, if it ever was. What Israel is pursuing, of former Israeli prime minister Ehud Olmert, is "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians," not from "some disproportionate outburst by some soldiers in some unit," but through "government policy--knowingly, evilly, irresponsibly dictated." And while campaigns of "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians" do not need to constitute genocide to morally necessitate opposition, there is a growing consensus--among those who know far more than we do about what is really happening in Gaza, and those who know far more than we do about genocide--that what Israel is doing in Gaza is, in fact, genocide. That is the conclusion of human rights organizations that have been operating on the ground there, from respected Israeli organizations like and to international organizations like , , and . And it is the conclusion of genocide experts like the of the International Association of Genocide Scholars and the of the Journal of Genocide Research. As Raz Segal, an Israeli professor of Holocaust and genocide studies at Stockton University, , "Can I name someone whose work I respect who does not think it is genocide? No, there is no counterargument that takes into account all the evidence." Or as Omer Bartov, another Israeli professor of Holocaust and genocide studies at Brown University, in The New York Times , "My inescapable conclusion has become that Israel is committing genocide against the Palestinian people. Having grown up in a Zionist home, lived the first half of my life in Israel, served in the I.D.F. as a soldier and officer and spent most of my career researching and writing on war crimes and the Holocaust, this was a painful conclusion to reach, and one that I resisted as long as I could. But I have been teaching classes on genocide for a quarter of a century. I can recognize one when I see one." "But what about the hostages?" you ask. Yes, Hamas still holds 50 hostages captive in Gaza. But there is simply no way in which a moral or rational person's evaluation of Israel's atrocities should be impacted--not by one single iota--by this sad reality. Because--and I really can't stress this enough--these are completely unrelated facts. They are unrelated in function, given that Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's administration has proven--again and again, beyond any shadow of a doubt, as articulated in the by the hostages' own families--that the Israeli government is not interested in freeing the hostages, and thus is not conducting its terror campaigns in Gaza in order to do so. Indeed, the man Netanyahu recently tried to install as the head of Israel's domestic intelligence agency literally , "I oppose hostage deals. This is a forever war." And they are unrelated in scale. Yes, it is tragic that 50 humans are being held hostage by Hamas. But that's less than half the number of humans that Israel while they were seeking food aid over just this past week. Yes, it is painful to imagine the fear and hunger being experienced right now by those fifty hostages. But Israel is essentially condemning the entire population of Gaza to comparable degrees of fear and hunger. If anything, the hunger Israel is inflicting on Gaza's two million human beings is worse: I have not heard of any hostages starving to death, which is what Israel has already done to , and is in the to tens or even hundreds of thousands more. You also cannot brush away these facts by pointing to some other part of the world where people are being violently oppressed. Even if we were to assume, as you and many others often argue, that Israel receives disproportionate attention for the atrocities it commits, that doesn 't in any way negate the fact that it is committing atrocities, nor does it diminish the urgent moral necessity of trying to end those atrocities. But we actually shouldn't assume that the attention Israel is currently receiving is disproportionate to its crimes. I don't think there is anywhere in the world right now where people are facing more intense or widespread oppression than Gaza. Certainly, if I had to send my two sons, your grandsons, into a conflict zone, there is nowhere I would fear more for them than Gaza--not the Xinjiang region of China; not Sudan; not the Democratic Republic of the Congo; literally nowhere. What's more, none of the violence being committed against civilians in these other conflicts is directly paid for by my tax dollars as an American, or carried out in my name as a Jew. As such, I should be giving this conflict my disproportionate attention, because I have disproportionate culpability in its commission. This genocide is an all-of-government effort, and polling shows it is largely backed by Israelis. A recent Haaretz found that 82 percent of Jewish Israelis support the expulsion of residents of the Gaza Strip to other countries, with 54 percent indicating they were "very" supportive; 56 percent of them even supported the forced expulsion of Arab citizens of Israel to other countries; and, most chilling of all, 47 percent of Jewish Israelis said they agreed that the IDF, "when conquering an enemy city, should act in a manner similar to the way the Israelites acted when they conquered Jericho under the leadership of Joshua, namely, to kill all its inhabitants." Yes, there are Israelis--including our Israeli family members--who strenuously oppose what is being done in Gaza (and who, it should be noted, are doing far more to protest Israel's government than me or most of us here in America). But it is clear that they represent a disturbingly . The reality is that Israeli society has, to a concerningly widespread degree, been radicalized, such that it is beginning to display characteristics of the societies that have facilitated genocides in the past--including, to just come right out and say it, the society that committed genocide against our people. And this gets to the real crux of my letter. Growing up, every aspect of my Jewish identity was connected to the Holocaust: through stories we were told by Grandpa about his childhood in Germany; the trip we took with him to Treblinka and Auschwitz; books we read like Maus and The Diary of Anne Frank ; movies we watched like Schindler 's List and Life Is Beautiful ; songs we sang like "Ani Ma'amin" and "I Still Believe"; visits we made to the Holocaust Museum and Yad Vashem; and a million other everyday comments at Sunday School, Hebrew School, Passover, family get-togethers, and more. In so many ways, it was instilled in me that there's nothing worse than supporting the perpetrators of genocide, nothing more cowardly than staying silent in the face of genocide, and nothing more virtuous than standing up against genocide. And I'm so grateful for this! It's a credit to you and our family and our Jewish community that "Never Again" was the morality that I was marinated in as a child and that has influenced so many of my choices as an adult. It's why as a state legislator I worked to pass--alongside an Armenian-American colleague--a for my state's public schools. It's why I've that reminded me of America's failure to accept Jewish refugees during the 1930s. It's why I spent an evening in jail for engaging in that had been operating much like a concentration camp. This has been a through-line not just in my career, but in the activism and worldview of so many Jewish Americans who were raised right by our parents. So when we see those same parents, the people we love most in the world, fail in this most elemental task--to clearly and unequivocally and loudly oppose a genocide (or even, if you still can't accept that terminology, an "indiscriminate, limitless, cruel and criminal killing of civilians" that strongly rhymes with genocide), let alone one being done in our name --that is not something we can just agree to disagree about. When I look at my two sons, I can't not see the children of Gaza--scared, orphaned, disfigured, and now literally starving to death--and feel profound shame that I have not done more over these past two years to stand against these crimes. Mom, you are a beautifully kind and empathetic person in so many ways. And yet, I don't believe that when you look at your grandsons, you have ever pictured them similarly. But I need you to try. I need you to step out of your echo chamber for a moment. I need you to at least consider the possibility that what Israel is doing to Gaza is, in practice if not scale, what was done to our ancestors. In short, I need you to be the parent I have always looked up to. The role model who taught me right from wrong. The mother who taught me to say "Never Again." Love, Your son, Aaron ? | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Aaron Regunberg ? ?

# Back to School Guide
---
| |
---
| Although HB 1586 passed during the 89th Regular Legislative Session, **printable exemptions** won’t be available for at least another month. Until then, parents must still [request exemption affidavits ](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTYxMjYxODcyMTI3OTM2MTAxLFwiclwiOjE2MTI2MTkwNTIwMjEyMDY1MH0iLCJzIjoiMTRlMGZlZDVjY2QwNzQwZSJ9)through the Department of State Health Services. This process can take **several weeks** \- especially during peak back to school season - so it’s critical to submit your request as soon as possible to avoid your child being excluded from school once classes begin! **New to the exemption process?** Don’t worry - [TFVC has you covered](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTYxMjYxODcyMTM0MjI3NTYyLFwiclwiOjE2MTI2MTkwNTIwMjEyMDY1MH0iLCJzIjoiNWRhMjk1MTc4YzM5OGYyZSJ9)! We’ve got everything you need to understand the steps, including how to handle notarization. **Want to connect with like-minded parents while checking this off your to-do list?** Join us at one of our upcoming [Back to School events](https://xdzgmz.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/cl/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEyMDEyNjAsXCJsXCI6MTYxMjYxODcyMTQxNTY3NjAzLFwiclwiOjE2MTI2MTkwNTIwMjEyMDY1MH0iLCJzIjoiOGRhZjdmMmY2YzgyN2RkMiJ9), where you can meet others in your community and receive _free notary services_ on site!

clean_body: | | The first day of school is around the corner | | | | | | Back to School Guide | | | Although HB 1586 passed during the 89th Regular Legislative Session, printable exemptions won’t be available for at least another month. Until then, parents must still the Department of State Health Services. This process can take several weeks \- especially during peak back to school season - so it’s critical to submit your request as soon as possible to avoid your child being excluded from school once classes begin! New to the exemption process? Don’t worry - ! We’ve got everything you need to understand the steps, including how to handle notarization. Want to connect with like-minded parents while checking this off your to-do list? Join us at one of our upcoming , where you can meet others in your community and receive free notary services on site! | | | | News | | | | | | TFVC vs Seminole ISD | TFVC President Rebecca Hardy and Political Director Michelle Evans were honored to represent countless families last week in Gaines County at a hearing in our lawsuit against Seminole ISD. Team TFVC will always fight for medical freedom in every corner of Texas. | | | Upcoming Events | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Follow us on Social Media | | | | We love getting social! We share events, action alerts, and pertinent news on all our channels. Come join the conversations by following and engaging today! | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- | TFVC In Action | | | | | | Lubbock Meet and Greet | Local liberty in action! Rebecca and Michelle had a great time connecting with passionate advocates at our Lubbock meetup last week. Thank you to everyone who showed up to stand for medical liberty in West Texas! | | | | Back to School OC Events | Team TFVC showed up strong this weekend! From farmers markets to back-to-school events across the state, our Outreach Coordinators were on the ground helping families understand their medical liberty rights and get their paperwork ready for the first day of school | | | | | Texans for Vaccine Choice 1540 Keller Parkway Ste 108 #166, KellerTexas, United States of America | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | You are receiving this email because you donated or signed up. |

name: AMAC Action
subject: A ticking time-bomb set by Joe Biden and Kamala Harris
body:
It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math.

| |
---
| | It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math.
---
| |
---
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zogfn18dt07k96ofrti80bgviokb93q6ag4e34q0_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)
---
| | **“****A disproportionate number of illegal immigrants go to blue states, they amplify the effect of a blue state vote. My understanding is that Democrats would lose approximately 20 seats in the house if illegals were not counted in census. And that’s also 20 less electoral votes for president.**** ** **So, illegals absolutely do impact who controls the House and the presidency.”****~** Elon Musk
---
| | Fellow Conservative,Elon Musk is right. It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. It’s also a ticking time-bomb set by Joe Biden and Kamala Harris – in the form of 11.5 million illegals entering our country on their watch – one which will explode shortly after President Trump leaves office in 2029.While you and I have no doubt President Trump will deliver on his promise to secure the border, this threat will not go away. The truth is, without immediate action, these illegal aliens will be counted in the U.S. Census, which determines both the apportionment of seats in the House of Representatives _and_ the number of electoral votes given to each state. Whether they came because, four years ago, Kamala Harris and Joe Biden prompted them to “surge to the border” for asylum or because of blue-states offering sanctuary status, “free” health care, “free” cell phones, “free” housing, and “free” college...it doesn’t matter. The result is the same.Your vote is _already_ being **watered down** in Congress. Your vote is _already_ being **watered down** in the Electoral College.And every day reports are popping up of non-citizens, illegal aliens, and convicted felons being registered to vote in cities and entire swing-states across the country, which means your vote may be fully **canceled out in future elections**. **_American elections should be decided by American citizens._****[If you agree then please sign your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution so the chaos we’re seeing now _never_ happens again in America.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zoc80tlsvthc50f712opkce6vub3u005j2f144q8_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)**Because, here’s the scary part:The last census took place in 2020. That was _before_ the 11.5 million illegal aliens who invaded our country under Joe Biden and Kamala Harris. To put it in context, there are only 8 states in America with a population over 11 million. So, it’s as if the radical Left just imported the 9th largest state in America. That’s more than North Carolina (10.8 million), Michigan (10 million), New Jersey (9.2 million), or Virginia (8.7 million).You get the picture. That’s why urgent action is needed to pass a Constitutional Amendment to ban illegal aliens from voting and from being counted for purposes of congressional and electoral vote apportionment. The next census in less than 6 years away. If those 11.5 million illegal aliens are counted in the census or are allowed to vote in the intervening years, you and I simply won’t have a country. **[It’s vitally important that your representatives in Washington and in your state legislature hear this demand directly from you.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zk1br3jueu9b3mqtvuvgidt8ek46os3cv9h41oj0_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)** Because, quite honestly, they think they can pull a fast one on you. They think you and I will be so relieved to see the border secure again and criminal illegal aliens deported, that you and I will be oblivious to this threat lying dormant under our very eyes.No way.My name is Rebecca Weber. I’m the chairwoman of AMAC Action. **AMAC Action is the most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative Seniors.****We’ve got 72 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates, who are the “feet on the street” advocates who contact Members of Congress and state legislators in their district offices.** **Moreover, our chapters and delegates help organize Town Hall meetings with elected officials and candidates to enable those locally of our 2,000,000 AMAC members to meet, hear, and question them.****And you should also know that we now have more than 337,000 AMAC members who respond to our “calls-to-action” and weigh in with members of the** **U.S. House and Senate, as well as their state legislators on the issues.** Quite simply, when we speak, Washington listens. [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z3425oo9lbgskt7opbfha4oht2gs38hcbt3jnsk8_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)**[And AMAC Action needs your help today in this do-or-die battle to pass a Constitutional Amendment to protect your vote from being canceled out by an illegal alien or a convicted felon.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z2md638qgkmq5c2vn54ads86tt1avu8vle0pvgcg_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)** But I can’t do it without you. **No matter what issue you care about the most, if you and I don’t have a secure,_honest_ elections... none of it matters.**Inflation. Illegal immigration. Violent Crime. Drug use. Homelessness. The 2nd Amendment. Out of control spending. Social Security and Medicare insolvency. Right to Life. Indoctrination in our schools. Endless foreign wars. You name it. **[So please follow this link to sign your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z3h37kohqd1p6rpl0as6k5sldpfgd4hf55085tu8_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)** so that AMAC Action can put your representatives in Washington and in the state legislature on notice with a simple demand: Namely, that you will not tolerate this erosion of your vote.Now, here’s the bad news:Most state constitutions grant voting rights to “every” citizen, open-ended language that liberal activists argue permits localities to authorize non-citizen and even illegal alien voting. States should amend their constitutions to instead permit “only” citizens to vote. But there’s good news, too:Seven states have _expressly_ banned non-citizen voting already: Alabama, Arizona, Colorado, Florida, Louisiana, North Dakota, and Ohio.**What’s more, voters approve of constitutional amendments to ban non-citizen voting by overwhelming margins. Nearly 77% of Ohio voters approved a measure in 2022, while nearly 63% of Colorado voters approved one in 2020.** This is a solution whose time has come. You and I can do this. **[That’s why, when you’ve signed your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zmh5o0tq9pn56fpi0vhs49l8mipud1qatq41sp28_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs), I'm also asking you to make a one-time contribution of $35 to give AMAC Action the resources to send out, collect, process, and tabulate millions of petitions.** The reality is, we don’t get a dime of taxpayer funding. Our funding comes from grassroots conservatives like you who care deeply about the future of America. This is important. _It means we can’t be bought._ On the other hand, the leftwing election fraud machine is funded by leftwing billionaires like Mark Zuckerberg and George Soros who are actively funneling _billions_ to dark money “non-profit” groups to “**reshape** ” our elections and our electorate.With that said, if you’re able to make a larger contribution – say $50, $75, $125, $250, $500, or even $1,000 – it would really be a life saver. Please hurry. In order to secure your vote in all future elections – and with 11.5 million illegals chomping to be included in the next census – **[your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution is vitally important.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z6u4a8v3ecc148lf23o1glr1nlb3u4tgs3j4lh10_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)** Yes, this is about saving your vote from being canceled. It’s also about saving America.And it all starts with the straightforward premise that non-citizens and illegal aliens – no matter how many Kamala Harris and Joe Biden imported on their watch – should be banned from voting _and_ banned from being counted for apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes. Thank you in advance for joining the fight to secure your vote
---
[ **SIGN THE PETITION**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zrqkab2a71kt361td45cscp8gv94aiia8jtc5mo0_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)
---
| | For Freedom,
---
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z1nufoipvrijc69e06m7s72brclum0e4bupm9da8_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)
---
**Rebecca Weber,_Chairwoman_****AMAC Action**
---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zdniih8u8fkr188utlpkopfppppn6kqidcla4ong_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)
---
| | P.S. American elections should be decided by American citizens. It’s not rocket-science. And it shouldn’t be controversial. Yet, the radical left is pouring billions into their scheme to “**reshape** ” our elections and our electorate. Nothing less than an amendment to the U.S. Constitution will suffice when it comes to stopping this threat. [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z3sro05bem2ivufbu62j2qsm2femmuuqg5cj4bn0_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs) **[So please sign your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution right away.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zldi2ess5u9ojaqbnvjj6foften3r8aibdavpi0g_rp22sh2s8i6epb6c3g6epb0cjh2bs)** P.P.S. clean_body: It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. | | | | It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. | | | | “A disproportionate number of illegal immigrants go to blue states, they amplify the effect of a blue state vote. My understanding is that Democrats would lose approximately 20 seats in the house if illegals were not counted in census. And that’s also 20 less electoral votes for president. So, illegals absolutely do impact who controls the House and the presidency.”~ Elon Musk | | Fellow Conservative,Elon Musk is right. It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. It’s also a ticking time-bomb set by Joe Biden and Kamala Harris – in the form of 11.5 million illegals entering our country on their watch – one which will explode shortly after President Trump leaves office in 2029.While you and I have no doubt President Trump will deliver on his promise to secure the border, this threat will not go away. The truth is, without immediate action, these illegal aliens will be counted in the U.S. Census, which determines both the apportionment of seats in the House of Representatives and the number of electoral votes given to each state. Whether they came because, four years ago, Kamala Harris and Joe Biden prompted them to “surge to the border” for asylum or because of blue-states offering sanctuary status, “free” health care, “free” cell phones, “free” housing, and “free” college...it doesn’t matter. The result is the same.Your vote is already being watered down in Congress. Your vote is already being watered down in the Electoral College.And every day reports are popping up of non-citizens, illegal aliens, and convicted felons being registered to vote in cities and entire swing-states across the country, which means your vote may be fully canceled out in future elections. American elections should be decided by American citizens., here’s the scary part:The last census took place in 2020. That was before the 11.5 million illegal aliens who invaded our country under Joe Biden and Kamala Harris. To put it in context, there are only 8 states in America with a population over 11 million. So, it’s as if the radical Left just imported the 9th largest state in America. That’s more than North Carolina (10.8 million), Michigan (10 million), New Jersey (9.2 million), or Virginia (8.7 million).You get the picture. That’s why urgent action is needed to pass a Constitutional Amendment to ban illegal aliens from voting and from being counted for purposes of congressional and electoral vote apportionment. The next census in less than 6 years away. If those 11.5 million illegal aliens are counted in the census or are allowed to vote in the intervening years, you and I simply won’t have a country. Because, quite honestly, they think they can pull a fast one on you. They think you and I will be so relieved to see the border secure again and criminal illegal aliens deported, that you and I will be oblivious to this threat lying dormant under our very eyes.No way.My name is Rebecca Weber. I’m the chairwoman of AMAC Action. AMAC Action is the most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative Seniors.We’ve got 72 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates, who are the “feet on the street” advocates who contact Members of Congress and state legislators in their district offices. Moreover, our chapters and delegates help organize Town Hall meetings with elected officials and candidates to enable those locally of our 2,000,000 AMAC members to meet, hear, and question them.And you should also know that we now have more than 337,000 AMAC members who respond to our “calls-to-action” and weigh in with members of the U.S. House and Senate, as well as their state legislators on the issues. Quite simply, when we speak, Washington listens. But I can’t do it without you. No matter what issue you care about the most, if you and I don’t have a secure,honest elections... none of it matters.Inflation. Illegal immigration. Violent Crime. Drug use. Homelessness. The 2nd Amendment. Out of control spending. Social Security and Medicare insolvency. Right to Life. Indoctrination in our schools. Endless foreign wars. You name it. so that AMAC Action can put your representatives in Washington and in the state legislature on notice with a simple demand: Namely, that you will not tolerate this erosion of your vote.Now, here’s the bad news:Most state constitutions grant voting rights to “every” citizen, open-ended language that liberal activists argue permits localities to authorize non-citizen and even illegal alien voting. States should amend their constitutions to instead permit “only” citizens to vote. But there’s good news, too:Seven states have expressly banned non-citizen voting already: Alabama, Arizona, Colorado, Florida, Louisiana, North Dakota, and Ohio.What’s more, voters approve of constitutional amendments to ban non-citizen voting by overwhelming margins. Nearly 77% of Ohio voters approved a measure in 2022, while nearly 63% of Colorado voters approved one in 2020. This is a solution whose time has come. You and I can do this. , I'm also asking you to make a one-time contribution of $35 to give AMAC Action the resources to send out, collect, process, and tabulate millions of petitions. The reality is, we don’t get a dime of taxpayer funding. Our funding comes from grassroots conservatives like you who care deeply about the future of America. This is important. It means we can’t be bought. On the other hand, the leftwing election fraud machine is funded by leftwing billionaires like Mark Zuckerberg and George Soros who are actively funneling billions to dark money “non-profit” groups to “reshape ” our elections and our electorate.With that said, if you’re able to make a larger contribution – say $50, $75, $125, $250, $500, or even $1,000 – it would really be a life saver. Please hurry. In order to secure your vote in all future elections – and with 11.5 million illegals chomping to be included in the next census – Yes, this is about saving your vote from being canceled. It’s also about saving America.And it all starts with the straightforward premise that non-citizens and illegal aliens – no matter how many Kamala Harris and Joe Biden imported on their watch – should be banned from voting and banned from being counted for apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes. Thank you in advance for joining the fight to secure your vote | | For Freedom, Rebecca Weber,ChairwomanAMAC Action | | | P.S. American elections should be decided by American citizens. It’s not rocket-science. And it shouldn’t be controversial. Yet, the radical left is pouring billions into their scheme to “reshape ” our elections and our electorate.
